close_reason: Guarded BigInt midpoint calculation on lines 27 and 115 to fall back to event.start when end is undefined (point events)
description: handleEventChanges() at line 27 and findNearestEvent() at line 115 use `/ 2n` BigInt division, but point events have `end === undefined`. Subtracting BigInt from undefined throws 'Cannot mix BigInt and other types'. This fires on every store dispatch after focus is set, cascading to break: Tab navigation (focusNext/focusPrevious), search close on Escape (hide() dispatch crashes before display:none runs, search bar stuck open), zoom button callbacks (dispatch triggers same subscriber crash). Root cause: lines 27 and 115 assume all events have BigInt `end` values.
title: BigInt TypeError in focus-manager.js crashes keyboard navigation

description: The `<nav aria-label='Timeline events'>` is positioned at y=492 with 1px height, while the canvas covers y=0 to y=493. The canvas intercepts all pointer events on the nav buttons. Screen reader users who use pointer devices (mouse, touch) cannot click event buttons. The nav's buttons time out on click because the canvas element sits on top. Needs either `pointer-events: none` on the canvas over that region, a higher z-index on the nav, or repositioning so buttons are not occluded.
title: Event nav buttons blocked by canvas pointer events

description: Zoom control buttons show `outline: none` in computed resting styles. Need to verify that `:focus` and `:focus-visible` CSS rules provide a visible focus ring that meets WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.7 (Focus Visible). If no focus styles are defined, add a visible outline/ring on keyboard focus for all interactive controls (help button, zoom buttons, filter toggle).
title: Verify visible focus indicators on zoom control buttons

description: The category filter panel container has no `role` or `aria-label` attribute. It renders as a generic `<div>` which gives screen readers no semantic context. Should be `role='region'` with `aria-label='Category filter'` or similar, so users understand the panel's purpose when navigating landmarks.
title: Filter panel missing ARIA role and label

close_reason: Lowered MAX_LANE_HEIGHT from 120px to 40px so lanes don't balloon with sparse data, reducing the visual disparity between point and range events
description:
getDynamicLaneConfig in lane-positioning.js expands lane height up to MAX_LANE_HEIGHT=120px to fill available vertical space. With few lanes (1-2), this creates two problems:

**~90% empty canvas**: Events cluster in the bottom 10% near the axis. The upper canvas is unused dark space. Event Layout spec says 'layout SHALL use screen space efficiently'.

**Extreme point vs range disparity**: ISS Expedition 1 (range event) renders as ~25x120px cyan tower while adjacent point events are 4x4px dots in the same lane. The area ratio is ~500:1. Evidence in point-vs-range-contrast.png and iss-range-event.png.

The dynamic height is well-intentioned (filling space) but backfires with sparse data because:
- Point events don't scale with lane height (they stay 4px)
- The height expansion mostly adds whitespace inside the lane around the point event
- Range events dominate visually despite not being more important

Possible approaches: cap effective lane height lower for sparse datasets, scale point event size with lane height, or position lanes more centrally in the canvas rather than anchored to the bottom axis.
title: Dynamic lane height wastes canvas space and creates visual imbalance

description:
Point events render as 4-8px squares depending on LOD (getMinEventWidth in lod.js). This violates multiple spec requirements:

**Accessibility spec - Touch Target Sizes**: 'touch targets SHALL be at least 24x24 CSS pixels, 44x44 preferred'. Current point events are 4x4px visual and 4px-wide hit target in hit-detection.js:47.

**Core Rendering spec - Event Visual Rendering**: 'point events SHALL be displayed as vertical markers AND markers SHALL be visually distinct from span events'. Current: small squares, not vertical markers.

**Event Layout spec - Micro view rendering**: 'all events SHALL be visible AND full detailed labels SHALL be shown'. Labels are gated by MIN_EVENT_WIDTH_FOR_LABEL=30 in label-collision.js:10, so point events (4-8px wide) NEVER get labels at any zoom level.

Evidence: At day-level micro zoom, SpaceX (May 2020) is a ~4px blue dot with no label, no tooltip reachable without pixel-precise hovering. 7 of 8 sample events are point events and none ever show labels.

Fix areas: lod.js getMinEventWidth, label-collision.js MIN_EVENT_WIDTH_FOR_LABEL, hit-detection.js point event width, renderer.js point rendering.
title: Point events too small to see, click, or label (spec violations)

description: The 'Skip to search' skip navigation link has href='#' which doesn't point to any element. When activated it navigates to page top instead of the search input. The search bar is dynamically created so it needs an id assigned, and the skip link should point to it or trigger the search bar to open.
title: Skip to search link has broken href="#" target

description: There are 4 skip links rendered but 2 are identical 'Skip to timeline' links pointing to #timeline-canvas. The duplicate appears to be an extra `<a>` element outside the skip-links `<nav>`. One should be removed to avoid confusing screen reader users who encounter the same link twice.
title: Duplicate 'Skip to timeline' link in skip navigation
